description:
**Problem**
Remaining CLI commands (upgrade/store/migrate) require host-runtime integrations in beads-rs, blocking full command tree migration to beads-cli without introducing crate cycles.

**Design**
Define a typed backend trait in beads-cli for host-coupled operations (upgrade orchestration, offline store admin fallback, migrate git integration). Implement the trait in beads-rs and inject into beads-cli dispatch.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Typed backend trait defined in beads-cli (no stringly APIs)
- [ ] beads-rs implements trait with existing behavior
- [ ] No crate dependency cycle introduced
- [ ] cargo check --workspace passes

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/*, crates/beads-rs/src/cli/*, crates/beads-rs/src/{upgrade,store_admin,git}*

notes.author: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
notes.content: Completed: added typed host backend seam in beads-cli and implemented in beads-rs with no behavior change. Added crates/beads-cli/src/backend.rs (CliHostBackend + typed request/response types), crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs impl, injected backend into CLI context, and rewired upgrade/store/migrate handlers to use trait calls. Validation: cargo check -p beads-cli; cargo check -p beads-rs; cargo check --workspace.

notes.author: darin@darinsmcstudio2.lan
notes.content: Completed: added typed host backend seam in beads-cli and implemented in beads-rs with no behavior change. Added crates/beads-cli/src/backend.rs (CliHostBackend + typed request/response types), crates/beads-rs/src/cli/backend.rs impl, injected backend into CLI context, and rewired upgrade/store/migrate handlers to use trait calls. Validation: cargo check -p beads-cli; cargo check -p beads-rs; cargo check --workspace.

title: Add typed CLI backend trait for upgrade/store/migrate host operations

description: The open() method uses check-then-unwrap pattern that could use entry API for cleaner code. Not a safety issue since we just inserted if not present.
title: git_worker.rs:78 unwrap can use entry API instead

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Remote-only rollback guidance has a working pre-cutover restore-handle recipe.
- [ ] Local-only rollback covers remote-only rehearsal by deleting the newly materialized local store ref when there was no preexisting local store.
- [ ] Fleet rollout guidance includes explicit fetch/readback verification for pushed cutovers.
- [ ] Dry-run wording and no-origin/no-op outcome semantics match current implementation.
description:
**Problem**
The updated rollout docs are still unsafe in a few concrete ways. The rollback recipe does not work for supported remote-only migration cases because it assumes a preexisting local store ref backup. The fleet loop does not perform the explicit fetch/readback verification used in the per-repo playbook. The docs also still describe dry-run as 'no writes' / 'never mutates refs' even though current implementation inspects remote state, and they do not account for the already-canonical no-origin no-op outcome.

**Files**: MIGRATION.md, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Rewrite the operator guidance so remote-only repos can create a restore handle from the remote store ref before cutover, local-only rollback covers the remote-only rehearsal case by deleting the materialized local store ref when appropriate, and fleet rollout includes an explicit fetch/readback verification step.

Align the dry-run wording with actual semantics after .21 lands, and document the no-origin/no-op outcome so fleet-success checks cannot misclassify it.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 89325899: `MIGRATION.md` now has a working remote-only rollback story and stronger fleet verification guidance. Operators can create a pre-cutover restore handle from `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` when no local store ref exists, remote-only `--no-push` rehearsals now document local rollback by deleting the materialized local store ref, fleet rollout now includes explicit fetch/readback verification of the pushed store ref, and the preview wording now states the real contract: dry-run does not mutate the caller repo's refs but may contact origin to inspect live remote state. Verification: docs were aligned to the passing migration behavior exercised by `cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture` and the full `cargo test` suite.

title: Migration follow-up: fix remote-only rollback and fleet verification docs

description:
**Problem**
Wire serialization/parsing lacks property-level coverage. Corrupted or unexpected data could silently lose fields.

**Design**
Add proptest-based roundtrip tests:
- serialize_state/parse_state
- serialize_tombstones/parse_tombstones
- serialize_deps/parse_deps
- serialize_meta/parse_meta (including last_write_stamp optional)
Additionally test tolerant parsing of legacy/extra fields (if applicable).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Roundtrip property tests added
- [ ] Meta last_write_stamp preserved
- [ ] Tests run fast in CI

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs, Cargo.toml
title: Wire format roundtrip + fuzz tests

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] CRDT_OVERHAUL.md updated to reflect current wire encoding.\n- [ ] Docs explicitly call out map vs vec and field names used in CBOR.
description:
**Problem**
CRDT_OVERHAUL.md describes DVV and dot encoding shapes that differ from the current implementation (map vs vec, field names).

**Files**: CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
design: **Design**\nUpdate docs to match actual on-wire encoding (CBOR/json) for WireDvvV1/WireDotV1 and any other drift discovered. Keep spec statements aligned with code to avoid future mismatches.
title: Cleanup: update CRDT_OVERHAUL.md wire format details
